message:
feat(conformance,integrate,fft): expand E2E test coverage, benchmarks, and IVP integration API

Significantly expand conformance test suites, performance benchmarks, and
the integration (IVP solver) API to improve scipy parity coverage and
regression detection.

Conformance:
- Expand E2E tests across all subsystems: FFT (+263), sparse (+222),
  CASP (+367), IVP (+765), special functions (+251)
- Expand evidence tests for P2C-001 through P2C-008 proof obligations
- Improve golden journey tests with broader scenario coverage
- Refine CI gates, quality gates, and forensics analysis

Integration (IVP solver):
- Expand integration API with additional solver configurations (+212 lines)
- Improve Runge-Kutta solver step tracking
- Refine step size control with improved tolerance handling
- Add validation enhancements for solver inputs
- Expand differential integration test coverage

FFT:
- Improve FFT library internals and transform implementations
- Expand FFT benchmarks with additional workloads

Benchmarks:
- Expand optimizer, sparse, and special function benchmarks
- Improve benchmark methodology and reporting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -54,6 +54,10 @@ pub enum IntegrateValidationError {
     MaxStepMustBePositive,
     AtolWrongShape { expected: usize, actual: usize },
     AtolMustBePositive,
+    NonFiniteY0,
+    NonFiniteF0,
+    TEvalOutOfSpan,
+    TEvalNotSorted,
     NotYetImplemented { function: &'static str },
 }
 
@@ -65,6 +69,10 @@ impl std::fmt::Display for IntegrateValidationError {
             Self::MaxStepMustBePositive => write!(f, "`max_step` must be positive."),
             Self::AtolWrongShape { .. } => write!(f, "`atol` has wrong shape."),
             Self::AtolMustBePositive => write!(f, "`atol` must be positive."),
+            Self::NonFiniteY0 => write!(f, "`y0` must be finite in Hardened mode."),
+            Self::NonFiniteF0 => write!(f, "`f0` must be finite in Hardened mode."),
+            Self::TEvalOutOfSpan => write!(f, "Values in `t_eval` are not within `t_span`."),
+            Self::TEvalNotSorted => write!(f, "Values in `t_eval` are not properly sorted."),
             Self::NotYetImplemented { function } => {
                 write!(f, "`{function}` is planned but not implemented yet.")
             }
